Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: portfolio-aware 8:30 AM pre-market brief

Previously the 8:30 AM brief generated ONE market-wide message and
blasted the same text to everyone. Claude had no idea what you held.

Now: one API call per portfolio. Claude receives your specific holdings
(symbol, quantity, avg cost, current price, P&L%) and generates a brief
that explicitly addresses each position — HOLD / ADD / TRIM / EXIT with
reasoning specific to today's market. Available cash is shown so the
'best setup today' recommendation is sized to your actual capital.

The 9:30 AM signal generation still uses the first portfolio's thesis
as global pre-market context (unchanged behaviour for signal gen).

commit.message:
fix: clone chat conversations not being saved to database

Root cause: `createConversation()` was never called after user login,
so `currentConversationId` stayed null and `saveMessages()` silently
returned without saving.

Fixes:
- Auto-create conversation when user is logged in but has no conversation
- Move save calls outside setMessages() callback for proper async handling
- Add error logging to saveMessages() so failures are visible in console

commit.message:
feat(api,ui): full environment scoping (CAB-1665) (#1443)

## Summary
- Add `?environment=` query param to consumers, applications, and
subscriptions API endpoints (backward compatible, None = all)
- Alembic migration adds nullable `environment` column to 3 tables
(consumers, portal_applications, subscriptions)
- Wire `useEnvironment` into Console Consumers, Applications,
Subscriptions pages for auto-refetch on env switch
- Wire `usePortalEnvironment` into Portal MyApplications page
- 8 new backend tests + 3 EnvironmentContext test mocks

Completes the environment scoping vision started in CAB-1664 (PR #1439).
Selecting "Staging" now filters data everywhere.

## Test plan
- [x] Backend: 116 tests pass (8 new env filter tests)
- [x] Console: 1935/1935 tests pass (ESLint 105/105)
- [x] Portal: MyApplications 12/12 tests pass, lint clean
- [x] TypeScript: both Console and Portal `tsc --noEmit` clean
- [ ] CI green

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Revise pottery wheel animation with realistic physics

- 3-frame cycling (200ms/frame) shifts pot-body rows by -9px/-18px per frame using CSS translateY with SVG clipPath masking
- Per-frame wavy-band shimmer: translateX(0/1.5/3px) + scaleY(1/1.02/1.1)
- New wheelSpin CSS keyframe applied to wheel-base group with variable animationDuration
- Deceleration sequence on stop: 2.4s → 3.6s → 5s → 8s → paused over 5 seconds
- useEffect + setInterval for frame cycling, useRef for cleanup

commit.message:
feat: persist TMDB auto-match candidates in the database

Adds a library_file_candidates table that stores pre-computed TMDB
matches per file. Matches survive between sessions so the import modal
can pre-populate them instantly.

Backend:
- Migration 00016: library_file_candidates table (composite PK on
  library_id + file_path; ON CONFLICT preserves existing TMDB data)
- ScanDisk() upserts candidates and merges stored matches into the
  returned DiskFile list, then fires a background TMDB matching pass
- Scan() (reconcile job) also triggers background matching
- matchCandidates() searches TMDB sequentially for unmatched candidates
  using the same title-normalise + year-must-match logic as the frontend
- Import handler deletes the candidate row on successful import
- library.NewService() accepts an optional tmdbSearcher (nil = disabled)

Frontend:
- DiskFile type gets optional tmdb_match field
- Import modal pre-populates match + autoMatched from DB data instantly
- Live sequential TMDB lookup (useEffect) skips rows already matched

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Overrides granulares por usuario (PermisoModulo) + modal de módulos

OVERRIDES INDIVIDUALES
- _calcular_perfil_overrides() ahora aplica 2 capas:
    Capa 1: PerfilAcceso (base del rol)
    Capa 2: PermisoModulo (override personal — prioridad sobre el perfil)
  - conceder: ve el módulo aunque el perfil no lo incluya
  - revocar:  no ve aunque el perfil sí lo incluya
  - reset:    elimina override, vuelve a la regla del perfil

NUEVAS VISTAS
- accesos_detalle_usuario(personal_pk) → JSON con perfil+overrides+efectivo por módulo
- accesos_toggle_modulo()              → POST conceder|revocar|reset + invalida cache RBAC

MODAL EN ACCESOS_GESTION
- Botón [sliders] por usuario abre modal con tabla de módulos
- Columnas: Módulo | Perfil base | Override | Efectivo | Acciones
- Íconos ✓/✗, badges Concedido/Revocado, color de fila según estado
- Botones + / − / ↺ con feedback AJAX sin recargar la página
- Re-renderiza la tabla al vuelo tras cada cambio

commit.message:
Remove series detection from PDF recommendations prompt

Pattern-based series detection was too aggressive — grouping unrelated
videos by shared 2-word prefixes (e.g. "How To", "The Best") into false
series. This caused recommendations to over-index on series analysis
with wrong categorizations. Series data is better suited for the
dedicated Series Analysis tab, not quick PDF recommendations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: install commands, agents, and MCPs without module name prefix (#55)

* feat: install commands, agents, and MCPs without module name prefix

Skills were already installed without a prefix. This change extends
that to commands, agents, and MCP server names, so installed files
use their natural names (e.g. `review-pr.md`, `@helper`) rather than
module-scoped names (e.g. `mymod.review-pr.md`, `@mymod.helper`).

Uninstall falls back to the old prefixed filename if the unprefixed
file is not found, keeping existing installs cleanly removable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove remaining prefix references in install.py update path

Five display strings in _update_commands, _update_agents, _update_mcps,
and _remove_orphaned_commands/agents still showed the old
/{module}.{cmd} and @{module}.{agent} format.

Also fixed two functional bugs:
- _remove_orphaned_mcps called remove_mcps without mcp_names, so no
  orphaned MCP servers were ever actually removed
- uninstall_cmd called remove_mcps without mcp_names for the same reason
- current_mcps was being built with the old {module}-{name} prefix,
  causing incorrect orphan detection during lola update

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add integration tests for no-prefix install behavior

32 end-to-end tests covering install → verify → uninstall / update
across all four assistants with no file-operation mocking.

Scenarios:
- Install creates bare filenames (no module. prefix)
- Agent frontmatter carries correct name/model/mode fields
- MCP keys in .mcp.json and opencode.json are bare server names
- Uninstall removes files, deletes empty MCP configs, clears registry
- Uninstall legacy fallback removes old module.cmd.md prefixed files
- lola update removes orphans, installs new items, updates registry
- installed.yml stores commands/agents/mcps without prefixes

New fixtures in conftest.py:
- integration_module: rich test module with skill, commands, agent, MCPs
- integration_env: fully isolated environment (no real ~/.lola touched)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  to name-based removal semantics; clarify module_name is unused

commit.message:
Add --importance_mode weight_variance + informative reparam logging

Weight×variance importance: I_k = ||W_fc2[:,k]|| · σ_k combines
downstream weight sensitivity with activation variance (zero-shot,
no reparam needed). Enabled via --importance_mode weight_variance.

Reparam logging: train_one_epoch returns per-component aux losses,
epoch summaries show reg/ent breakdown, log_channel_stats adds
aggregate V-norm summary across all layers, initial V-norms logged
at reparameterization time as baseline.

Fixed _residual_weight usage in log_channel_stats aggregate section
(was broken for NormalizedResidualManager which uses v_tilde not v).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(security): comprehensive audit fixes across 8 subsystems

Multi-agent deep audit reviewed ~50 files. Fixes 13 confirmed bugs:

Security (MEDIUM):
- Multipart content_type CRLF injection: sanitize CR/LF/NUL in file part content-type
- HTTP header NUL byte acceptance: reject control chars per RFC 9110 (decode+encode paths)
- SCRAM timing side-channel: use constant-time comparison for server signature verification
- ParsedUrl userinfo rejection: prevent host header confusion from user@host URLs
- Cross-origin redirect credential stripping: remove Authorization/Cookie on origin change

Protocol correctness (MEDIUM):
- gRPC Metadata::get() case-insensitive: use eq_ignore_ascii_case per HTTP/2 semantics
- CORS preflight Vary header: use append_vary_header instead of clobbering .insert()
- PostgreSQL bind message overflow: i32::try_from() guard on parameter value length
- PgTransaction Drop: set needs_rollback flag, auto-ROLLBACK before next query/execute
- HTTP extension method validation: add is_valid_token() filter per RFC 9110

Reliability (MEDIUM):
- Actor on_stop cancellation masking: mask cancel during cleanup (matching GenServer)
- Process wait_async: exponential backoff (1ms→50ms) instead of busy-loop yield_now

Protocol completeness (LOW):
- Add 11 missing HTTP status reason phrases (101, 202, 303, 307, 308, 409, 410, etc.)
- NUL byte filtering in escape_content_disposition_value

Modules found sound (0 bugs): sync primitives (mutex/rwlock/pool), I/O extensions,
browser streams, TLS acceptor, lab runtime, HTTP/2 settings, happy eyeballs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add runner saturation policy automation

## What Changed

Added automated recovery system that monitors and responds to CI gridlock:

### Runner Saturation Policy (`runner-saturation-policy.sh`)
- Monitors queue depth every 5 minutes (cron-ready)
- Auto-detects thresholds: HEALTHY (<100), ELEVATED (<200), CRITICAL (<300), GRIDLOCK (>300)
- Auto-enables MERGE_SURGE mode when CRITICAL
- Auto-cancels archived workflows when CRITICAL
- Emergency cancels ALL runs when GRIDLOCK
- Generates status reports in `docs/ci/recovery-status-update.md`

### Thresholds & Actions

**HEALTHY** (<100 queued):
- No action required

**ELEVATED** (100-199 queued):
- Monitor only, no intervention

**CRITICAL** (200-299 queued):
- Auto-enable MERGE_SURGE mode
- Cancel archived workflow runs
- Preserve new 8-workflow system

**GRIDLOCK** (>300 queued):
- Emergency: Cancel ALL queued runs
- Enable MERGE_SURGE mode
- Alert for manual intervention

### First Run Results

**Detected**: GRIDLOCK (500 queued)
**Actions Taken**:
- ✅ Enabled MERGE_SURGE mode
- ✅ Cancelled 200 queued runs
- 📊 Queue reduced: 500 → 100 (80% reduction)
- 🎯 Status: GRIDLOCK → WARNING

### Recovery Status

Current file: `docs/ci/recovery-status-update.md`
- Auto-updated by saturation policy
- Shows queue depth, actions taken, monitoring commands
- Timestamped for tracking recovery trends

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Inject prefers-color-scheme dark overrides into light-theme Mermaid SVGs

After rendering, inject a <style>@media (prefers-color-scheme:dark){...}</style>
block into each SVG produced from a light-background page. Attribute-value
selectors override the hardcoded light fills/strokes with a palette derived
from Tufte's #151515 bg using the same lighten_hex offsets. Dark-frontmatter
pages are skipped (their fills are already dark). Removes completed item from
ROADMAP and renumbers the remaining items.
